title: Hardcoded System Path Not Portable
description: Path `/home/mario/projects/wheelwright/mywheel/WAI-Harness` is hardcoded and assumes mario user and specific directory structure. Breaks on other systems or user accounts.
impact: Deployment scripts will fail silently on non-matching systems; harness_upgrade.py won't trigger for users with different home directories.
recommendation: Use environment variable or relative path resolution. Extract to PROJECT_DIR/WAI-Harness reference or read from configuration.

title: Em-dash Pattern in Error Messages
description: Multiple error messages use em-dash (—) character in stderr output. Pattern matches CLAUDE.md anti-pattern warning for em-dashes in shell contexts, even though stderr is not corrupted.
impact: Inconsistent with project anti-pattern guidelines; sets bad precedent for future error handling code.
recommendation: Replace em-dashes with double-hyphen (--) or plain hyphens (-) in all error messages for consistency with shell best practices.

title: Missing Git Availability Checks
description: Multiple git invocations (git rev-parse HEAD, git status --short) lack checks for git availability. If git is not in PATH, subprocess calls fail silently.
impact: Silent failures in session startup; wakeup brief generation may be incomplete; anomaly detection skipped.
recommendation: Add `command -v git >/dev/null 2>&1` checks before git operations; provide fallback behavior when git unavailable.

title: Complex Shell-to-Python Interface Fragility
description: Python code embedded in bash heredoc with variable interpolation. Subprocess calls (git rev-parse, git diff) within Python lack error handling; if git fails, exceptions propagate but may not be caught by shell layer.
impact: Subprocess failures can crash hook execution; session initialization may abort with unhelpful error messages.
recommendation: Wrap subprocess calls in try/except with explicit exit codes; test error paths on systems without git.

title: Unvalidated JSON Reads in wai-enter.sh
description: JSON extraction from EXPEDITER_STATE file uses Python fallback to empty string on error (line 43). If file is malformed, the fallback silently hides the problem.
impact: Malformed JSON in scan_state.json files won't trigger alerts; feeds refresh may be incorrectly skipped.
recommendation: Add explicit validation: parse JSON, check for required fields, log warnings on parse failures instead of silent fallback.
